title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Speed Booster Test

questions.question: The bases of a hemisphere and a cone are equal. If their height is also the same, then what is the ratio between their curved surface areas?
questions.explanation: Given: Radius of hemisphere (r) = Radius of cone (r). Height of hemisphere = Height of cone (h). Formula Used: Height of hemisphere = r Slant height of cone (l) = r2+h2 \sqrt{r^2 + h^2}r2+h2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Curved Surface Area of hemisphere = 2ŌÄr22ŌÄr^22ŌÄr2‚Äč‚Äč Curved Surface Area of cone = ŌÄrl Solution: Since heights are equal, the height of the cone (h) = radius (r). Slant height of the cone (l) = r2+r2=2r2=2r \sqrt{r^2 + r^2} = \sqrt{2r^2} = \sqrt{2}rr2+r2‚Äč=2r2‚Äč=2‚Äčr‚Äč‚Äč Curved Surface Area of the hemisphere = 2ŌÄr22ŌÄr^22ŌÄr2‚Äč‚Äč Curved Surface Area of the cone = ŌÄr(2r)=2ŌÄr2ŌÄr(\sqrt{2}r) = \sqrt{2}ŌÄr^2ŌÄr(2‚Äčr)=2‚ÄčŌÄr2‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = 2ŌÄr22ŌÄr2\frac{2ŌÄr^2}{\sqrt{2}ŌÄr^2}2‚ÄčŌÄr22ŌÄr2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 22=21=2:1 \frac{2}{\sqrt{2}} = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{1} = \sqrt{2} : 12‚Äč2‚Äč=12‚Äč‚Äč=2‚Äč:1‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/d762f702-5488-440e-ad48-93d82a2024f9.png)

questions.question: During a sale, 55% of the goods are sold at a 41% profit, 20% of the remaining goods are sold at a 19% profit and the rest are sold at a 21% loss. If there is an overall profit of x%, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Given 55% goods sold at 41% profit 20% of remaining goods sold at 19% profit Rest sold at 21% loss Formula Used Net Profit/Loss % = Weighted average of individual profit/loss percentages. Solution Let total goods be 100. Part 1: 55 goods sold at +41% Remaining goods = 100 - 55 = 45 Part 2: 20% of 45 = 9 goods sold at +19% Part 3: Rest = 45 - 9 = 36 goods sold at -21% Total net profit = 55√ó41+9√ó19+36√ó(‚ąí21)55 \times 41 + 9 \times 19 + 36 \times (-21)55√ó41+9√ó19+36√ó(‚ąí21)‚Äč‚Äč Total net profit = 2255 + 171 - 756 = 1670 Overall percentage = 1670100=16.7%\frac{1670}{100} = 16.7\%1001670‚Äč=16.7%‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (d) Exam Hall Approach: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b8c97074-24aa-4ab5-8264-5431d1c0940d.png)

questions.question: From the top of a tower, the angles of depression of two obwects, on the ground on the same side of it are observed to be 60¬į and 30¬į, respectively, and the height of the tower is 450 m. The distance between the obwects (in m) is:
questions.explanation: Given Height of tower (h) = 450 m Angles of depression = 60‚ąėand30‚ąė60^{\circ} and 30^{\circ}60‚ąėand30‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used ‚Äčtan‚Ā°(őł)=PerpendicularBase\tan(\theta) = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Base}}tan(őł)=BasePerpendicular‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Let the distances of the obwects from the base of the tower be x and y (where y > x). ‚ÄčFor angle 60‚ąė:tan‚Ā°(60‚ąė)=450x 3=450x\text{For angle}\, 60^{\circ}: \tan(60^{\circ}) = \frac{450}{x} \\\ \\\sqrt{3} = \frac{450}{x}For angle60‚ąė:tan(60‚ąė)=x450‚Äč 3‚Äč=x450‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčx=4503=1503mx = \frac{450}{\sqrt{3}} = 150\sqrt{3} mx=3‚Äč450‚Äč=1503‚Äčm‚Äč‚Äč For angle 30‚ąė:tan‚Ā°(30‚ąė)=450y30^{\circ}: \tan(30^{\circ}) = \frac{450}{y}30‚ąė:tan(30‚ąė)=y450‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč13=450y y=4503m\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} = \frac{450}{y} \\\ \\y = 450\sqrt{3} m3‚Äč1‚Äč=y450‚Äč y=4503‚Äčm‚Äč‚Äč Distance between obwects = y - x Distance = 4503‚ąí1503=3003m450\sqrt{3} - 150\sqrt{3} = 300\sqrt{3} m4503‚Äč‚ąí1503‚Äč=3003‚Äčm‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: If cot A = 34\frac 3443‚Äč‚Äč, and A is an acute angle, then find the value of sin2A - cos3A.
questions.explanation: Given cot A = 34\frac{3}{4}43‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč A is an acute angle Formula Used: sin 2A = 2sin A cos A cos 3A = 4cos3A‚ąí3cosA4cos^3 A - 3cos A4cos3A‚ąí3cosA‚Äč‚Äč Solution Since cot A =34 \frac{3}{4}43‚Äč‚Äč, Base = 3, Perpendicular = 4 Hypotenuse = 5 sin A = 45\frac{4}{5}54‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč cos A =35 \frac{3}{5}53‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč sin 2A = 2 √ó 45√ó35=2425=120125\frac{4}{5} √ó \frac{3}{5} = \frac{24}{25} = \frac{120}{125}54‚Äč√ó53‚Äč=2524‚Äč=125120‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč cos 3A = 4(35)3‚ąí3(35)4(\frac{3}{5})^3 - 3(\frac{3}{5})4(53‚Äč)3‚ąí3(53‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč cos 3A = 4(27125)‚ąí954(\frac{27}{125}) - \frac{9}{5}4(12527‚Äč)‚ąí59‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč cos 3A = 108125‚ąí225125=‚ąí117125\frac{108}{125} - \frac{225}{125} = -\frac{117}{125}125108‚Äč‚ąí125225‚Äč=‚ąí125117‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč sin 2A - cos 3A = 120125‚ąí(‚ąí117125)=120+117125=237125\frac{120}{125} - (-\frac{117}{125})= \frac{120 + 117}{125} = \frac{237}{125} \\\\125120‚Äč‚ąí(‚ąí125117‚Äč)=125120+117‚Äč=125237‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: A mixture contains 15% of substance A and 85% of substance B. if you add more of aa so that the new mixture contains 35% A, by what percentage did A increases?
questions.explanation: Given: Initial substance A = 15% Initial substance B = 85% New substance A = 35% Formula Used: Concentration = (PartTotal)√ó100 (\frac{Part }{ Total}) \times 100(TotalPart‚Äč)√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let initial mixture be 100 units. Then A = 15 units, B = 85 units. Let x units of A be added. New mixture total = 100 + x New A = 15 + x ‚Äč15+x100+x=35100 15+x100+x=720\frac{15 + x}{100 + x} = \frac{35}{100}\\ \ \\\frac{15 + x}{100 + x} = \frac{7}{20}100+x15+x‚Äč=10035‚Äč 100+x15+x‚Äč=207‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč 300 + 20x = 700 + 7x 13x = 400 x = 40013‚Čą30.769\frac{400}{13} \approx 30.76913400‚Äč‚Čą30.769‚Äč‚Äč Percentage increase in A = =x15√ó100 =4001315√ó100 =8039√ó100‚Čą205.13%= \frac{x}{15} \times 100 \\ \ \\= \frac{\frac{400}{13}}{15} \times 100 \\ \ \\= \frac{80}{39} \times 100 \approx 205.13\%=15x‚Äč√ó100 =1513400‚Äč‚Äč√ó100 =3980‚Äč√ó100‚Čą205.13%‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: What is the maximum value that must be assigned to A so that the 8-digit number 713A8845 is divisible by 3?
questions.explanation: Given Number = 713A8845 Condition = Divisible by 3 Formula Used A number is divisible by 3 if the sum of its digits is divisible by 3. Solution Sum of digits = 7 + 1 + 3 + A + 8 + 8 + 4 + 5 Sum = 36 + A For 36 + A to be divisible by 3, A can take values 0, 3, 6, 9. The maximum possible value for a single digit A is 9. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Tewasvi, Trishana and Meena are three sisters. Tewasvi and Trishana are twins. The sum of the ages of Tewasvi and Trishana is the same as that of Meena. Four years earlier, the ratio of the ages of Tewasvi and Meena was 1:3. What will be the age of Trishana 4 years from now?
questions.explanation: Given Tewasvi (T) and Trishana (Tr) are twins T = Tr T + Tr = Meena (M) Ratio 4 years earlier = 1 : 3 Solution Since T = Tr, T + T = M 2T = M Four years ago: ‚ÄčT‚ąí4M‚ąí4=13\frac{T - 4}{M - 4} = \frac{1}{3}M‚ąí4T‚ąí4‚Äč=31‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč 3(T - 4) = M - 4 3T - 12 = 2T - 4 T = 8 Trishana's present age = 8 years Trishana's age 4 years from now = 8 + 4 = 12 years Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) ‚Äč Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/7e4b0cdb-b9e9-44c4-a3ed-68242de7449f.png)

questions.question: The average of 150 numbers is 75. It is found that while calculating the average, two numbers 71 and 16 were wrongly read as 17 and 61. What is the average if the readings of these two numbers are recorded correctly now?
questions.explanation: Given Total numbers = 150 Incorrect average = 75 Correct numbers = 71 and 16 Wrong numbers = 17 and 61 Formula Used Correct Sum = Incorrect Sum - Wrong Values + Correct Values Correct Average = Correct SumTotal numbers \frac{\text{Correct Sum}}{\text{Total numbers}}Total numbersCorrect Sum‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Incorrect Sum = 150√ó75=11250150 \times 75 = 11250150√ó75=11250‚Äč‚Äč Correct Sum = 11250 - 17 - 61 + 71 + 16 Correct Sum = 11250 - 78 + 87 Correct Sum = 11250 + 9 = 11259 Correct Average = 11259150\frac{11259}{150}15011259‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Correct Average = 75.06 Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a16f5740-60d6-4c24-821b-fae7db077493.png) ‚Äč

questions.question: A sum of money triples itself at a certain rate of compound interest in 3 years. In how many years will it amount to 27 times of itself?
questions.explanation: Given: A sum triples itself in 3 years at compound interest. Formula Used: If a sum becomes xxx times in ttt years at compound interest, it will become xnx^nxn times in n√ótn \times tn√ót years. Solution: The sum becomes 313^131 times in 3 years. We need to find the time it takes to become 27 times. We can write 27 as 333^333. Here, n=3n = 3n=3. Time taken to become 333^333 times = 3√ó3=93 \times 3 = 93√ó3=9 years. Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If sinx = cos(3x-20¬į), what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčsin‚Ā°x=cos‚Ā°(3x‚ąí20‚ąė)\sin x = \cos(3x - 20^{\circ})sinx=cos(3x‚ąí20‚ąė)‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: ‚Äčsin‚Ā°őł=cos‚Ā°(90‚ąė‚ąíőł)\sin \theta = \cos(90^{\circ} - \theta)sinőł=cos(90‚ąė‚ąíőł)‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Using the complementary angle identity: ‚Äčcos‚Ā°(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=cos‚Ā°(3x‚ąí20‚ąė)\cos(90^{\circ} - x) = \cos(3x - 20^{\circ})cos(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=cos(3x‚ąí20‚ąė)‚Äč‚Äč Equating the angles: ‚Äč90‚ąė‚ąíx=3x‚ąí20‚ąė90^{\circ} - x = 3x - 20^{\circ}90‚ąė‚ąíx=3x‚ąí20‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč90‚ąė+20‚ąė=3x+x90^{\circ} + 20^{\circ} = 3x + x90‚ąė+20‚ąė=3x+x‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč110‚ąė=4x110^{\circ} = 4x110‚ąė=4x‚Äč‚Äč x = 110‚ąė4=27.5‚ąė \frac{110^{\circ}}{4} = 27.5^{\circ}4110‚ąė‚Äč=27.5‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If a + 1/a = 4, then 4a2+46a2+10a+6\frac{4a^2+4}{6a^2+10a+6}6a2+10a+64a2+4‚Äč‚Äč=?
questions.explanation: Given: a + 1a\frac{1}{a}a1‚Äč‚Äč = 4 Solution: Expression is: = 4a2+46a2+10a+6\frac{4a^2 + 4}{6a^2 + 10a + 6}6a2+10a+64a2+4‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Divide the numerator and the denominator by a = 4a+4a6a+10+6a\frac{4a + \frac{4}{a}}{6a + 10 + \frac{6}{a}}6a+10+a6‚Äč4a+a4‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Take out common factors = 4(a+1a)6(a+1a)+10\frac{4(a + \frac{1}{a})}{6(a + \frac{1}{a}) + 10}6(a+a1‚Äč)+104(a+a1‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Substitute the given value = 4√ó46√ó4+10\frac{4 √ó 4}{6 √ó 4 + 10}6√ó4+104√ó4‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 1624+10\frac{16}{24 + 10}24+1016‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 1634 \frac{16}{34}3416‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 817\frac{8}{17}178‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Evaluate: (8.85√ó78.3225+1.15√ó1.3225)(79.645‚ąí10.1775)=?\frac{(8.85√ó78.3225+1.15√ó1.3225)}{(79.645-10.1775)}=?(79.645‚ąí10.1775)(8.85√ó78.3225+1.15√ó1.3225)‚Äč=?‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given Expression = 8.85√ó78.3225+1.15√ó1.322579.645‚ąí10.1775\frac{8.85 \times 78.3225 + 1.15 \times 1.3225}{79.645 - 10.1775}79.645‚ąí10.17758.85√ó78.3225+1.15√ó1.3225‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used ‚Äča3+b3=(a+b)(a2‚ąíab+b2)a^{3} + b^{3} = (a + b)(a^{2} - ab + b^{2})a3+b3=(a+b)(a2‚ąíab+b2)‚Äč‚Äč Solution Let a = 8.85 and b = 1.15 Notice that 78.3225 = (8.85)2=a2(8.85)^{2} = a^{2}(8.85)2=a2‚Äč‚Äč Notice that 1.3225 = (1.15)2=b2(1.15)^{2} = b^{2}(1.15)2=b2‚Äč‚Äč The numerator becomes: ‚Äča√óa2+b√ób2=a3+b3a \times a^{2} + b \times b^{2} = a^{3} + b^{3}a√óa2+b√ób2=a3+b3‚Äč‚Äč In the denominator: ab=8.85√ó1.15=10.1775 a2+b2=78.3225+1.3225=79.645ab = 8.85 \times 1.15 = 10.1775 \\\ \\a^{2} + b^{2} = 78.3225 + 1.3225 = 79.645ab=8.85√ó1.15=10.1775 a2+b2=78.3225+1.3225=79.645‚Äč So the denominator is a2+b2‚ąíaba^{2} + b^{2} - aba2+b2‚ąíab‚Äč‚Äč The entire expression reduces to: ‚Äča3+b3a2‚ąíab+b2\frac{a^{3} + b^{3}}{a^{2} - ab + b^{2}}a2‚ąíab+b2a3+b3‚Äč‚Äč This simplifies to a + b Result = 8.85 + 1.15 = 10 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If ‚ą†P, ‚ą†Q, ‚ą†R and ‚ą†S of a quadrilateral PQRS, taken in order, are in the ratio 4:3:7:6, then quadrilateral PQRS will be of which type?
questions.explanation: Given Ratio of angles P : Q : R : S = 4 : 3 : 7 : 6 Formula Used Sum of interior angles of a quadrilateral = 360 degrees If consecutive interior angles are supplementary, lines are parallel. Solution Sum of the ratio parts = 4 + 3 + 7 + 6 = 20 Value of 1 part = 36020=18‚ąė \frac{360}{20} = 18^\circ20360‚Äč=18‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Angle P = 4√ó18=72‚ąė4 \times 18 = 72^\circ4√ó18=72‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Angle Q = 3√ó18=54‚ąė 3 \times 18 = 54^\circ3√ó18=54‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Angle R = 7√ó18=126‚ąė 7 \times 18 = 126^\circ7√ó18=126‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Angle S = 6√ó18=108‚ąė6 \times 18 = 108^\circ6√ó18=108‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Checking sum of adwacent angles: Angle P + Angle S =72‚ąė+108‚ąė=180‚ąė 72^\circ + 108^\circ = 180^\circ72‚ąė+108‚ąė=180‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Angle Q + Angle R = 54‚ąė+126‚ąė=180‚ąė54^\circ + 126^\circ = 180^\circ54‚ąė+126‚ąė=180‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč Since adwacent angles on the sides PS and QR are supplementary, the side PQ is parallel to the side RS. Therefore, exactly one pair of opposite sides is parallel, making it a trapezium. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: P can finish a certain work in the same time in which Q and R together can finish it. If P and Q together could finish the work in 20 days and R alone in 100 days, Q alone can finish the work in:
questions.explanation: Given: Time of P = Time of (Q + R) Time of (P + Q) = 20 days Time of R = 100 days Formula Used: Work done per day = 1Total Days \frac{1}{\text{Total Days}}Total Days1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: From the given time equality, their rates of work relate as: ‚Äč1P=1Q+1R\frac{1}{P} = \frac{1}{Q} + \frac{1}{R}P1‚Äč=Q1‚Äč+R1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč The combined rate of P and Q is: ‚Äč1P+1Q=120\frac{1}{P} + \frac{1}{Q} = \frac{1}{20}P1‚Äč+Q1‚Äč=201‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč The rate of R is: ‚Äč1R=1100\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{100}R1‚Äč=1001‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Substitute 1P\frac{1}{P}P1‚Äč‚Äč into the combined rate equation: ‚Äč(1Q+1R)+1Q=120(\frac{1}{Q} + \frac{1}{R}) + \frac{1}{Q} = \frac{1}{20}(Q1‚Äč+R1‚Äč)+Q1‚Äč=201‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč2Q+1100=120\frac{2}{Q} + \frac{1}{100} = \frac{1}{20}Q2‚Äč+1001‚Äč=201‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč2Q=120‚ąí1100 2Q=5100‚ąí1100=4100 2Q=125 Q=50 days\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{1}{20} - \frac{1}{100} \\ \ \\\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{5}{100} - \frac{1}{100} = \frac{4}{100}\\ \ \\\frac{2}{Q} = \frac{1}{25}\\ \ \\Q = 50 \text{ days}Q2‚Äč=201‚Äč‚ąí1001‚Äč Q2‚Äč=1005‚Äč‚ąí1001‚Äč=1004‚Äč Q2‚Äč=251‚Äč Q=50 days‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/3b5e15ba-42f8-45a6-a458-51b6c5d547ae.png)

questions.question: The simple interest for a sum of Rs. 6200 for 4 years is Rs. 80 more than the simple interest for Rs. 5800 for the same duration, the interest rate being same in both cases. What is the rate of interest?
questions.explanation: Given Principal 1 (P1) = Rs. 6200 Principal 2 (P2) = Rs. 5800 Time (T) = 4 years Difference in Simple Interest = Rs. 80 Formula Used ‚ÄčSI=P√óR√óT100SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}SI=100P√óR√óT‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Difference in SI = (P1‚ąíP2)√óR√óT100\frac{(P1 - P2) \times R \times T}{100}100(P1‚ąíP2)√óR√óT‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Difference in Principals = 6200 - 5800 = 400 The difference in Simple Interest is due to this difference in Principal over 4 years. ‚Äč80=400√óR√ó4100 80=16R R=8016 R=5%80 = \frac{400 \times R \times 4}{100} \\\ \\80 = 16R\\\ \\R = \frac{80}{16}\\\ \\R = 5 \%80=100400√óR√ó4‚Äč 80=16R R=1680‚Äč R=5%‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: Within a circle, two parallel chords are positioned such that the center lies between them if the distance between these chords is 14 cm , and the length of one chord is 12 cm having radius of 10 cm , what is the length of the other chord?
questions.explanation: Given: Radius of the circle, r = 10 cm Length of first chord, AB = 12 cm Distance between the two parallel chords = 14 cm Formula Used: Perpendicular distance from center to a chord bisects the chord. d = r2‚ąí(chord2)2 \sqrt{r^2 - (\frac{\text{chord}}{2})^2}r2‚ąí(2chord‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the distance from the center to the first chord be d1.d_1.d1‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčd1=102‚ąí(122)2d_1 = \sqrt{10^2 - (\frac{12}{2})^2}d1‚Äč=102‚ąí(212‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč d1=100‚ąí36=64d_1 = \sqrt{100 - 36} = \sqrt{64}d1‚Äč=100‚ąí36‚Äč=64‚Äč‚Äč = 8 cm The total distance between the chords is 14 cm, and the center lies between them. Let the distance from the center to the second chord be d2.d_2.d2‚Äč.‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčd1+d2=14d_1 + d_2 = 14d1‚Äč+d2‚Äč=14‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč8+d2=148 + d_2 = 148+d2‚Äč=14‚Äč‚Äč d2d_2d2‚Äč‚Äč = 6 cm Now, calculate the half-length of the second chord: ‚ÄčCD2=r2‚ąíd22\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{r^2 - d_2^2}2CD‚Äč=r2‚ąíd22‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚ÄčCD2=102‚ąí62\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{10^2 - 6^2}2CD‚Äč=102‚ąí62‚Äč‚Äč CD2=100‚ąí36=64\frac{\text{CD}}{2} = \sqrt{100 - 36} = \sqrt{64}2CD‚Äč=100‚ąí36‚Äč=64‚Äč‚Äč = 8 cm The total length of the second chord is: CD = 2 √ó 8 = 16 cm Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A and B start a business by investing ‚āĻ29,000 and ‚āĻ95,000, respectively. B withdraws his investment after 6 months, and then C woins the business with an investment of ‚āĻ67,000 for the remaining period of the year. If 15% of the total profit is ‚āĻ6,600, what is B‚Äôs share of the profit?
questions.explanation: Given A's investment = ‚āĻ29,000 for 12 months B's investment = ‚āĻ95,000 for 6 months C's investment = ‚āĻ67,000 for 6 months 15% of Total Profit = ‚āĻ6,600 Formula Used Profit Ratio = Ratio of (Investment √ó\times √ó‚ÄčTime) Solution Total Profit = 6,6000.15=44,000 \frac{6,600}{0.15} = 44,0000.156,600‚Äč=44,000‚Äč‚Äč Ratio of profits A : B : C = (29000√ó12):(95000√ó6):(67000√ó6)(29000 \times 12) : (95000 \times 6) : (67000 \times 6)(29000√ó12):(95000√ó6):(67000√ó6)‚Äč‚Äč Dividing by 6000: A : B : C = (29√ó2):(95√ó1):(67√ó1)=58:95:67 (29 \times 2) : (95 \times 1) : (67 \times 1) = 58 : 95 : 67(29√ó2):(95√ó1):(67√ó1)=58:95:67‚Äč‚Äč Total parts = 58 + 95 + 67 = 220 B's share =95220√ó44000= \frac{95}{220} \times 44000=22095‚Äč√ó44000‚Äč‚Äč B's share = 95√ó200=19,00095 \times 200 = 19,00095√ó200=19,000‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If the income of Raghu is 40% more than Kumar, then the income of Kumar is less than that of Raghu by what percent?
questions.explanation: Given Raghu's income is 40% more than Kumar's income Formula Used Percentage Less = DifferenceReference Value√ó100 \frac{\text{Difference}}{\text{Reference Value}} \times 100Reference ValueDifference‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution Let the income of Kumar be 100. Income of Raghu = 100+40% of 100=140100 + 40\% \text{ of } 100 = 140100+40% of 100=140‚Äč‚Äč Difference between their incomes = 140 - 100 = 40 We need to find how much less Kumar's income is compared to Raghu's. Percentage Less = 40140√ó100\frac{40}{140} \times 10014040‚Äč√ó100%‚Äč Percentage Less = 27√ó100%\frac{2}{7} \times 100 \%72‚Äč√ó100%‚Äč Percentage Less = 2007% \frac{200}{7} \%7200‚Äč%‚Äč‚Äč Percentage Less = 2847%28 \frac{4}{7} \%2874‚Äč%‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (c) Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/b11bcc35-31c2-404f-aebb-9a1043eae6fa.png)

questions.question: A cistern has two inlets I1 and I2 which can fill it in 16 hours and 20 hours, respectively. An outlet can empty the full cistern in 12 hours. If all the three pipes are opened together in the empty cistern, how much time will they take to fill the cistern completely?
questions.explanation: Given Time taken by I1 = 16 hours Time taken by I2 = 20 hours Time taken by outlet = 12 hours Formula Used Total Work = LCM of times Efficiency = Total WorkTime\frac{\text{Total Work}}{\text{Time}}TimeTotal Work‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Total capacity = LCM(16, 20, 12) = 240 units Efficiency of I1 = 24016\frac{240}{16}16240‚Äč‚Äč = +15 units/hour Efficiency of I2 = 24020\frac{240}{20} 20240‚Äč‚Äč= +12 units/hour Efficiency of outlet = 240‚ąí12 \frac{240}{-12}‚ąí12240‚Äč‚Äč = -20 units/hour Net efficiency = 15 + 12 - 20 = 7 units/hour Time taken to fill =2407=3427 \frac{240}{7} = 34 \frac{2}{7}7240‚Äč=3472‚Äč‚Äč hours Final Answer So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Arrange the following values in ascending order: ‚Äč174,413,412,194\frac{17}{4}, 4 \frac{1}{3}, 4 \frac{1}{2}, \frac{19}{4}417‚Äč,431‚Äč,421‚Äč,419‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given Fractions to be ordered: 174,413,412,and194\frac{17}{4}, 4\frac{1}{3}, 4\frac{1}{2}, and \frac{19}{4}417‚Äč,431‚Äč,421‚Äč,and419‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution Convert the given values to decimal form: ‚Äč174=4.25 413=133‚Čą4.333 412=4.5 194=4.75\frac{17}{4} = 4.25 \\\ \\4\frac{1}{3} = \frac{13}{3} \approx 4.333\\\ \\4\frac{1}{2} = 4.5\\\ \\\frac{19}{4} = 4.75417‚Äč=4.25 431‚Äč=313‚Äč‚Čą4.333 421‚Äč=4.5 419‚Äč=4.75‚Äč‚Äč Arranging them in ascending order (smallest to largest): 4.25 < 4.333 < 4.5 < 4.75 This corresponds directly to: ‚Äč174<413<412<194\frac{17}{4} < 4\frac{1}{3} < 4\frac{1}{2} < \frac{19}{4}417‚Äč<431‚Äč<421‚Äč<419‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Kiran buys an article at 12% below its cost and sells it at 12% above its cost. Find his profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Given: Buying price = 12% below original cost. Selling price = 12% above original cost. Formula Used: Profit = Selling Price (SP) - Actual Cost Price (CP) Profit % = ProfitActual CP\frac{Profit}{Actual\ CP}Actual CPProfit‚Äč‚Äč √ó 100 Solution: Let the original cost of the article be 100 units. Actual Cost Price (CP) for Kiran = 100 - 12% of 100 = 88 units. Selling Price (SP) = 100 + 12% of 100 = 112 units. Profit = SP - Actual CP = 112 - 88 = 24 units. Profit % =2488 \frac{24}{88}8824‚Äč‚Äč √ó 100 Profit % = 311√ó100=30011 \frac{3}{11} √ó 100 = \frac{300}{11}113‚Äč√ó100=11300‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Profit % = 27.27% Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A closed cylindrical tank with a height of 1 m and a base diameter of 140 cm must be constructed from a metal sheet. For the same, how many m¬≤ of the sheet are required? [Use ŌÄ = 22/7]
questions.explanation: Given: Height of cylinder (h) = 1 m Base diameter = 140 cm Formula Used: Radius (r) = Diameter2\frac{Diameter}{2}2Diameter‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Total Surface Area of closed cylinder = 2ŌÄr(h + r) Solution: Convert diameter to meters: 140 cm = 1.4 m Radius (r) = 1.42\frac{1.4}{2}21.4‚Äč‚Äč = 0.7 m Total Surface Area = 2 √ó 227√ó0.7√ó(1+0.7)\frac{22}{7} √ó 0.7 √ó (1 + 0.7)722‚Äč√ó0.7√ó(1+0.7)‚Äč‚Äč Area = 2 √ó 22 √ó 0.1 √ó 1.7 Area = 4.4 √ó 1.7 Area = 7.48 m¬≤ Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in the ratio of ‚ÖĎ:1/11:1/13 and its perimeter is 718 cm . Find the length of its longest side in metre.
questions.explanation: Given: Ratio of sides = ‚ÖĎ : 1/11 : 1/13 Perimeter = 718 cm Formula Used: Perimeter = Sum of all sides Solution: Find a common base for the ratio by multiplying with the LCM of 9, 11, and 13 (which is 1287): Ratio = 12879:128711:128713\frac{1287}{9} : \frac{1287}{11} : \frac{1287}{13}91287‚Äč:111287‚Äč:131287‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = 143 : 117 : 99 Let the sides be 143x, 117x, and 99x. Sum of the sides (Perimeter) = 143x + 117x + 99x = 359x We know the perimeter is 718 cm: 359x = 718 x = 2 The longest side corresponds to the largest ratio value (143): Longest side = 143 √ó 2 = 286 cm Convert the length to meters: 286 cm = 2.86 m Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If (x‚Äď3) is the HCF of x¬≤ ‚Äď 5x + 6 and x¬≤ ‚Äď kx + 9, then what is the value of k?
questions.explanation: Given Polynomial 1 = x2‚ąí5x+6x^{2} - 5x + 6x2‚ąí5x+6‚Äč‚Äč Polynomial 2 = x2‚ąíkx+9x^{2} - kx + 9x2‚ąíkx+9‚Äč‚Äč HCF of the polynomials = (x - 3) Formula Used Factor Theorem: If (x - a) is a factor of polynomial P(x), then P(a) = 0. Solution Since (x - 3) is the HCF, it must be a factor of x2‚ąíkx+9.x^{2} - kx + 9.x2‚ąíkx+9.‚Äč‚Äč Substitute x = 3 into the second polynomial and equate to zero. ‚Äč(3)2‚ąík(3)+9=09‚ąí3k+9=018‚ąí3k=03k=18k=6(3)^{2} - k(3) + 9 = 0 \\9 - 3k + 9 = 0\\18 - 3k = 0\\3k = 18\\k = 6(3)2‚ąík(3)+9=09‚ąí3k+9=018‚ąí3k=03k=18k=6‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A number when divided by a divisor leaves a remainder of 25. When twice the original number is divided by the same divisor, the remainder is 13. What is the value of the divisor?
questions.explanation: Given: Remainder when a number is divided by a divisor = 25 Remainder when twice the number is divided by the same divisor = 13 Formula Used: Dividend = Divisor √ó Quotient + Remainder Divisor = 2√óR1‚ąíR2 2 √ó R_1 - R_22√óR1‚Äč‚ąíR2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the number be N, divisor be D, and quotient be q. N = D √ó q + 25 When the number is doubled: 2N = 2(D √ó q + 25) = 2Dq + 50 When 2N is divided by D, the remainder will be the remainder when 50 is divided by D. Given remainder is 13. So, 50 = D √ó (some quotient) + 13 Since the remainder is 13, D must be greater than 13. 50 - 13 = D √ó (some quotient) 37 = D √ó (some quotient) Since 37 is a prime number, D must be 37. Final Answer So the correct answer is (b) ‚Äč Exam Hall Method: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/2b190b2f-7734-4f82-a27c-c9a042ba5ecb.png)
